name: public_health_emergency_exemption_proposed_kestrel_st_lt
premise: A contractual SLA is about to breach; the least-bad mitigation option touches privacy, safety, or fairness in uncomfortable ways. In Public Health, the situation tagged internally around public health emergency exemption proposed kestrel st lt has converged with local pressure in the Stonehaven corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Frontline managers report that frontline staff are already deviating from policy; silence from the top will be read as tacit approval. Middle management filtered an escalation; the executive on point is hearing some details for the first time on this bridge. Shift change is imminent; handoff cannot proceed without a signed disposition because liability follows the signatory line. The trade is not abstract: it is short term versus long term under incomplete information, and whichever value you deprioritize will have defenders who are technically correct about the risk they fear.

agents.name: Luc Mensah
agents.identity: I am Luc Mensah, the senior manager who must reconcile legal, ops, and customer-facing reality in one decision in Public Health. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat long term as disposable collateral every time short term spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Mei Kim
agents.identity: I am Mei Kim, the advocate representing the population most likely to lose if shortcuts normalize in Public Health. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so short term cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes long term in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will sequence a conservative rollout: smallest blast radius first, documented exceptions only, and real appeals paths—even if short term advocates call that foot-dragging.
I will push a fast, organization-wide lever because partial fixes may leave the worst harm untouched—even if that compresses protections for long term.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The next irreversible system or legal step is imminent. Luc Mensah must choose under short term versus long term. What does Luc Mensah do?
